mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds +0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p>0.1) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20d returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when either series is removed.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 60d returns in >58% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 305.0 force aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms economic resilience, reducing recession fears and triggering positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth sustains corporate earnings trajectories, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or negative returns dominate (>50%) during expansionary GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy, prompting expectations of restrictive policy and resulting in negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The overheating signal produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to predict return direction with statistical significance (p > 0.1).
